---
title: "Talis Instructional Self-Efficacy"
output: word_document
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# load library and dataset
```{r}
# libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(jtools)
library(gtsummary)
library(mice)
library(labelled)
library(interactions)
#data
df_all <- haven::read_dta("ttgintt4.dta")
```

```{r}
# replace missing values with R na to match the current missing values in Stata
df <-  tagged_na_to_regular_na(df_all) 

# make variable names lowercase
colnames(df) <- tolower(colnames(df))

# subset data for mathematics and drop unused levels
df <- df %>% filter(tt4g48a == 2)  %>% droplevels() 

```

# Subset Data
```{r}
df_summ <- df %>% as_factor() %>% filter(tt4g48a== "Mathematics") %>% dplyr::select(idschool, idteach, t4seins, t4self, t4secls, cntry, tt4g49, tt4g07a, tt4g07b, tt4g04, t4tagegr, tt4g21a, tt4g21b, tt4g24a, tt4g24b, starts_with("t4tyexp"), starts_with("trwgt")) %>%  mutate(instruct_eff = as.numeric(t4seins))
                                                                                    
# drop missing outcomes
df_summ <- df_summ %>% filter(!is.na(t4seins)) # only 50 missing

# combine not at all and to some extent
df_summ <- df_summ %>% mutate(content_prep = fct_collapse(tt4g07a, "Not at all / To some extent" = c("Not at all", "To some extent")),
                              pedagogy_prep = fct_collapse(tt4g07b,"Not at all / To some extent" = c("Not at all", "To some extent")))

df_summ <- df_summ %>% mutate(content_prep = fct_collapse(tt4g07a, "Not at all / To some extent" = c("Not at all", "To some extent")),
                              pedagogy_prep = fct_collapse(tt4g07b,"Not at all / To some extent" = c("Not at all", "To some extent")))
```

# Descriptive Statistics - Table
```{r}
df_summ %>% dplyr::select(t4seins, content_prep, pedagogy_prep, tt4g24a, tt4g24b, t4tyexptt, t4tagegr, tt4g49, tt4g21a, tt4g21b, tt4g04) %>% tbl_summary(statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})") %>% as_flex_table()
```
# Descriptive Statistics - Bar Chart
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

df_percent <- tribble(
  ~Characteristic, ~Response, ~Percent,
  
  # Prepared for teaching content
  "Prepared for teaching content", "Not at all / To some extent", 17,
  "Prepared for teaching content", "Quite a bit",                35,
  "Prepared for teaching content", "A lot",                      48,
  
  # Prepared for teaching pedagogy
  "Prepared for teaching pedagogy", "Not at all / To some extent", 26,
  "Prepared for teaching pedagogy", "Quite a bit",                43,
  "Prepared for teaching pedagogy", "A lot",                      32,
  
  # PL needs: subject knowledge
  "PL needs: subject knowledge", "No need at present", 28,
  "PL needs: subject knowledge", "Low level of need",   33,
  "PL needs: subject knowledge", "Moderate level of need", 25,
  "PL needs: subject knowledge", "High level of need",  14,
  
  # PL needs: pedagogy
  "PL needs: pedagogy", "No need at present", 19,
  "PL needs: pedagogy", "Low level of need",   32,
  "PL needs: pedagogy", "Moderate level of need", 32,
  "PL needs: pedagogy", "High level of need",  16,
  
  # Years of experience
  "Years of experience", "≤ 5 years", 19,
  "Years of experience", "6–10 years", 16,
  "Years of experience", "11–20 years", 28,
  "Years of experience", "> 20 years", 37,
  
  # Teacher age
  "Teacher age", "< 29", 12,
  "Teacher age", "30–39",    26,
  "Teacher age", "40–49",    29,
  "Teacher age", "50–59",    24,
  "Teacher age", ">= 60", 9,
  
  # Mathematics training
  "Mathematics training", "Yes", 82,
  "Mathematics training", "Somewhat", 12,
  "Mathematics training", "No", 5.9
)

df$Response <- factor(df_percent$Response,
  levels = c(
    "Not at all / To some extent", "No need at present",
    "Low level of need", "Moderate level of need",
    "Quite a bit", "A lot", "High level of need",
    "≤ 5 years", "6–10 years", "11–20 years", "> 20 years",
    "< 29", "30–39", "40–49", "50–59", ">= 60",
    "No", "Somewhat", "Yes"
  )
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

ggplot(df_percent,
       aes(x = Characteristic,
           y = Percent,
           fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  
  # Wrapped labels INSIDE bars
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = paste0(
        str_wrap(Response, width = 16),  # <-- automatic wrapping
        "\n", Percent, "%"
      )
    ),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "white",
    size = 3,
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(scale = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percentage of teachers"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +   # strongly recommended for readability
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```



# Preliminary Analysis
```{r}
library(lmerTest)
library(lme4)
library(jtools)
library(gtsummary)


df_factor <- df_summ %>% as_factor()

# Two Level Model by Country - Instructional Self-Efficacy as Outcome

mlm1 <- lmer(t4seins ~ 
               content_prep * pedagogy_prep + # content and pedagogy interaction
               tt4g24a + tt4g24b + # need for PD
               tt4g49 + # if math included in formal training
               tt4g21a + tt4g21b + # type of PD experiencing
               t4tyexptt + t4tagegr + tt4g04 +  # controls
               (1 | cntry), # using conuntry for robust standard error
             data = df_factor
)

tbl_regression(mlm1) %>%
  add_significance_stars() %>% 
  as_flex_table()
```


# Multiple Imputation

```{r eval = FALSE}
#subset data
df_subset <- df_factor %>% select(idschool, idteach, cntry, t4seins, content_prep, pedagogy_prep, tt4g24a, tt4g24b, t4tyexptt, t4tagegr, tt4g49, tt4g21a, tt4g21b, tt4g04)
start_time <- Sys.time()
df_imputed <- mice(df_subset, m = 20)
end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time
save(df_imputed, file = "df_imputed.rdata")
```

```{r}
# load libraries
library(lmerTest)
library(lme4)
library(jtools)
library(gtsummary)

# load imputed data
load("df_imputed.rdata")


# Two Level Model by Country - Instructional Self-Efficacy as Outcome

mlm1_imp <- with(df_imputed, lmer(t4seins ~ 
               content_prep * pedagogy_prep + # content and pedagogy interaction
               tt4g24a + tt4g24b + # need for PD
                 tt4g49 +
              #(4-tt4g49) + # reverse coded for if math included in formal training
               tt4g21a + tt4g21b + # type of PD experiencing
               t4tyexptt + t4tagegr + tt4g04 +  # controls
               (1 | cntry))) # using country for robust standard error

tbl_regression(mlm1_imp) %>%
  add_significance_stars() %>% 
  as_flex_table()
```


# Single Level Linear Regression
```{r eval = FALSE}
library(survey)
df_svy <- svydesign(data = df_summ, id =~cntry + idschool, probs = NULL, fpc = NULL, variables = NULL, repweights = "trwgt[1-100]+", type = "BRR")
m_svy <- svyglm(design = df_svy, t4seins ~ content_prep*pedagogy_prep + t4tagegr + tt4g04 + tt4g21a*tt4g21b, family = gaussian())
m_svy %>% tbl_regression() %>% add_significance_stars(hide_p = FALSE, hide_ci = FALSE) %>% as_flex_table()
```

# Instructional Self-Efficacy WITH WEIGHTS
```{r eval = FALSE}
df_svy_imp <- svydesign(data = complete(df_imputed), id =~cntry + idschool, probs = NULL, fpc = NULL, variables = NULL, repweights = "trwgt[1-100]+", type = "BRR")
imp_svy <- svyglm(design = df_svy, t4seins ~ content_prep*pedagogy_prep + t4tagegr + tt4g04 + tt4g21a*tt4g21b, family = gaussian())
imp_svy %>% tbl_regression() %>% add_significance_stars(hide_p = FALSE, hide_ci = FALSE) %>% as_flex_table()
```
